learn.title: Virtual Power Plants with AI
learn.intro: Virtual power plants aggregate millions of home batteries, EVs and solar inverters into one dispatchable resource. AI optimizes them in real time, reducing grid stress and fossil peaker use. This distributed model is vital for renewables integration as India adds 500 GW non-fossil capacity by 2030.
learn.c1_text: A virtual power plant links 1,000s of rooftop solar and batteries via software. Like a orchestra conductor, AI coordinates them to act as one power station. A 10 kW home system joined with 9,999 others creates 100 MW, enough to replace a small gas plant.
learn.c2_text: AI predicts solar output and demand every 5 minutes, shifting battery charge to sunset peaks. It bids aggregated power into markets, earning ₹4–8/unit. This balances grids without new transmission, cutting curtailment of renewable energy by up to 15% in pilot studies.
learn.c3_text: India's PM-KUSUM and smart meter push enable VPPs. Tata Power Delhi runs a 1 MW residential VPP. With 10 million smart meters by 2025, disaggregated DER can provide 5 GW of flexible capacity, aiding the national grid during heatwave evening peaks.
learn.stat2: VPP can cut peaker emissions by 30%

learn.title: Agrivoltaics: Solar Meets Farming
learn.intro: Agrivoltaics places solar panels above crops, sharing land for food and clean power. It cuts water loss and raises farmer income. With India's land pressure, dual-use farms can deploy 1,000 GWp solar without hurting agriculture, key to rural energy transition.
learn.c1_text: Panels at 3m height let tractors pass; like a patio umbrella, they shade soil. US NREL shows 3% land loss but 30% more crop value. A 1 acre system yields 250 MWh/yr plus chili harvest, doubling farmer earnings versus mono-crop.
learn.c2_text: Panel shade lowers ground temp 2–4°C, slashing evaporation 20–30%. Sensors show soil moisture stays higher. This lets solar-powered drip irrigation run longer, stabilizing yield during droughts while generating power above the same parcel.
learn.c3_text: Kerala and Rajasthan test agrivoltaics on 100 acres. MNRE's 2024 guidelines allow 33% panel coverage on farms. At Bikaner, a 5 MW pilot grows mustard under panels, giving farmers ₹40,000/acre extra, supporting 280 GW solar target on non-forest land.
learn.stat3: Water saved: up to 30% under panels

learn.title: Hydrogen Fuel Cells in Vehicles
learn.intro: Hydrogen fuel cells power EVs without batteries, emitting only water. They suit trucks and buses needing fast refuel. As India pushes green hydrogen, fuel-cell vehicles can decarbonize 10% of freight by 2030, complementing electric buses in clean transport.
learn.c1_text: A fuel cell stacks hydrogen and air across a membrane; like a battery fed by gas, it makes electricity. A 100 kW unit moves a bus 400 km. Only heat and water exit, vs 2.3 kg CO2/km for diesel, giving zero tailpipe pollution.
learn.c2_text: Stacks feed motor directly, refueling in 10 min vs 4 hr charge. Toyota Mirai uses 5 kg H2 for 650 km. In India, fuel-cell trucks on Delhi–Mumbai corridor could avoid 1.5 Mt CO2/yr, using green H2 from NTPC pilots at ₹300/kg.
learn.c3_text: National Green Hydrogen Mission funds 5 fuel-cell buses in Leh. IOCL runs H2 station in Faridabad. With ₹19,700 cr outlay, India targets 5 MMT green H2 by 2030, enabling 50,000 fuel-cell vehicles in mining and logistics fleets.

learn.title: Power Purchase Agreements Explained
learn.intro: PPAs let firms buy renewable power at fixed rates for 20–25 years, financing huge projects without upfront cost. They drove 40% of corporate RE globally. In India, PPAs unlocked 70 GW wind and solar, critical to meet 500 GW clean target.
learn.c1_text: A PPA is a 20-year promise: developer builds solar, sells at ₹2.5/unit. Like leasing a roof AC, buyer gets power, not panels. This backs 80% of India's 90 GW solar; risk shifts to developer, enabling ₹4 lakh cr investment since 2015.
learn.c2_text: Banks fund projects on PPA cashflows, lowering tariff 30% vs merchant. Global corporates signed 36 GW in 2022. In India, RE-RTC PPAs with storage now offer 24/7 power at ₹4/unit, attracting Foxconn and Amazon to Karnataka data centers.
learn.c3_text: SECIs reverse auction PPAs cut rates to ₹1.99/unit in Rajasthan. Open-access PPAs let firms like Tata Motors buy 100 MW directly. By 2024, 12 GW corporate PPA signed, supporting 280 GW 2030 goal and ₹11 lakh cr clean capex.

learn.title: Solar PV and Photoelectric Effect
learn.intro: Solar panels turn sunlight to power via the photoelectric effect, the basis of all PV. Understanding this helps India scale 90 GW installed and 280 GW by 2030, powering homes cleanly while cutting 1.2 Bt CO2 annually versus coal.
learn.c1_text: Sunlight hits silicon; like knocking balls off a table, photons free electrons. A 1 m2 panel gets 1 kW sun, yielding 200 W. Einstein explained this; each photon ejects one electron, creating DC at 0.5 V per cell in a typical 60-cell module.
learn.c2_text: Cells wired in series raise voltage to 30 V. Inverters flip DC to AC for grids. Bifacial panels grab reflected light, +10% yield. A 1 kW rooftop in Mumbai makes 1,400 kWh/yr, offsetting 1 tonne CO2 versus grid coal power.
learn.c3_text: Bhadla park (2.2 GW) uses 8M panels at ₹2/unit. PM Surya Ghar gives 10 GW rooftop by 2027. India's 90 GW PV avoids 120 Mt CO2/yr; domestic module making hit 40 GW capacity, supporting 280 GW 2030 clean target.

learn.title: Floating Solar on Water
learn.intro: Floating solar uses lakes and canals for PV, saving land and cutting water loss. India's 10 GW potential on reservoirs can power 3 million homes while reducing evaporation 40%, vital for water-stressed states hitting 280 GW RE goal.
learn.c1_text: Panels on pontoons cover 1% of a lake; like a lid on a pot, they block sun evaporation. A 100 MW float on a reservoir saves 1.2 bn liters/yr. Cooling lifts output 5–10% versus land PV, giving 160 MWh/acre annually.
learn.c2_text: HDPE floats anchor to shore, inverters on raft convert DC. At Ramagundam (100 MW), 4 lakh panels sit on a coal mine pit. Avoids 220 acres land, shows hybrid use; O&M uses boats, with 25-year life and 22% capacity factor.
learn.c3_text: NTPC plans 2 GW on dams; Kerala's 500 kW on canal saves paddy water. MNRE estimates 10 GW float potential. Pavagada-like projects free 5,000 acres; by 2030, 5 GW floating can cut 8 Mn tonnes CO2 and evaporation in 12 states.
